headline: Qualcomm (QCOM) Loses 8.8% on Nvidia Competition Fears
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (NASDAQ:QCOM) is one of the 10 Stocks Entering June in the Wrong Direction. headline: Why Is Nvidia (NVDA) Stock Soaring Today
summary: Shares of leading designer of graphics chips Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) jumped 6.3% in the afternoon session after over 150 million shares traded hands (well above average) as CEO Jensen Huang's keynote at Computex in Taipei delivered two major announcements.

headline: Why Is Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
summary: Shares of enterprise technology company Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) jumped 9.4% in the afternoon session after the company benefited from a sector-wide rally in artificial intelligence (AI) server stocks and announced a new server designed for AI workloads.

content.summary: Shares of enterprise technology company Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) jumped 9.4% in the afternoon session after the company benefited from a sector-wide rally in artificial intelligence (AI) server stocks and announced a new server designed for AI workloads.

headline: Microsoft And Nvidia RTX Spark Puts AI PCs In Investor Focus
summary: Nvidia and Microsoft have launched the RTX Spark AI superchip for Windows PCs, targeting AI powered personal computing. Spark enabled Windows devices from Microsoft, Dell, HP, Lenovo and others are expected to arrive this fall. The partnership aims to support local AI, personal agents running directly on devices, and new security features. For investors watching NasdaqGS:MSFT, the RTX Spark launch adds a fresh angle to the Windows story by linking the operating system directly to on device...

headline: Nvidia Expands Into CPUs As AI Partnerships Grow And Valuation Stretches
summary: Nvidia (NasdaqGS:NVDA) announced the RTX Spark superchip platform for Windows PCs and the Vera CPU for data centers, expanding into CPU markets that have traditionally centered on other large chip suppliers. The company introduced open source physical AI tools and agentic AI platforms, including Agent Toolkit, Cosmos, Alpamayo, Isaac Groot for robotics, and Vera Rubin for AI factories. Nvidia also disclosed new partnerships with Microsoft, MediaTek, Dell, TSMC, Uber, VinFast, and Foxconn,...

Nvidia also disclosed new partnerships with Microsoft, MediaTek, Dell, TSMC, Uber, VinFast, and Foxconn,...

headline: ServiceNow (NOW) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of enterprise workflow automation company ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) jumped 9.4% in the afternoon session after it continued to rally extending double digit gains from the previous session, which was driven by two forces: Snowflake's blowout Q1 results showing enterprise AI adoption is accelerating rather than disrupting software demand, and Dell's confirmation that enterprises are committing to full-stack AI infrastructure at a scale nobody had modelled.

content.title: ServiceNow (NOW) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
content.summary: Shares of enterprise workflow automation company ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) jumped 9.4% in the afternoon session after it continued to rally extending double digit gains from the previous session, which was driven by two forces: Snowflake's blowout Q1 results showing enterprise AI adoption is accelerating rather than disrupting software demand, and Dell's confirmation that enterprises are committing to full-stack AI infrastructure at a scale nobody had modelled.

headline: Why Qualcomm (QCOM) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
summary: Shares of wireless chipmaker Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) fell 7.7% in the afternoon session after competitor Nvidia unveiled its new RTX Spark superchip, a powerful processor for Windows PCs that directly challenges Qualcomm's Snapdragon series.

content.title: Why Qualcomm (QCOM) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
content.summary: Shares of wireless chipmaker Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) fell 7.7% in the afternoon session after competitor Nvidia unveiled its new RTX Spark superchip, a powerful processor for Windows PCs that directly challenges Qualcomm's Snapdragon series.

headline: Why Dell (DELL) Stock Is Trading Up Today
summary: Shares of computer hardware and IT solutions company Dell (NYSE:DELL) jumped 9.5% in the afternoon session after it continued a rally from the previous trading session as the company posted record-breaking fiscal first-quarter results driven by surging demand for its artificial intelligence (AI) servers.

content.title: Why Dell (DELL) Stock Is Trading Up Today
content.summary: Shares of computer hardware and IT solutions company Dell (NYSE:DELL) jumped 9.5% in the afternoon session after it continued a rally from the previous trading session as the company posted record-breaking fiscal first-quarter results driven by surging demand for its artificial intelligence (AI) servers.

headline: HPE surges 29% as AI infrastructure boom boosts server demand
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise shares rallied nearly 29% in premarket trade on Tuesday, as investors cheered ‌the AI server maker pulling forward its long-term financial ‌targets by two years, buoyed by strong AI infrastructure demand.  HPE, which competes with ​Dell Technologies and Super Micro Computer in enterprise servers, is seeing sustained demand as large enterprises bring forward purchases to avoid supply risks amid surging memory chip prices.  Hyperscalers including Alphabet and Amazon ‌are set to spend ⁠more than $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, likely boosting demand for HPE's server and networking ⁠products.

content.title: HPE surges 29% as AI infrastructure boom boosts server demand
content.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise shares rallied nearly 29% in premarket trade on Tuesday, as investors cheered ‌the AI server maker pulling forward its long-term financial ‌targets by two years, buoyed by strong AI infrastructure demand.  HPE, which competes with ​Dell Technologies and Super Micro Computer in enterprise servers, is seeing sustained demand as large enterprises bring forward purchases to avoid supply risks amid surging memory chip prices.  Hyperscalers including Alphabet and Amazon ‌are set to spend ⁠more than $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, likely boosting demand for HPE's server and networking ⁠products.

headline: Even Nvidia Will Struggle to Save the PC
summary: Chip stocks, led by Intel and  Qualcomm  fell sharply, while PC stocks, led by  Dell Technologies  and  HP  jumped.  A new kind of PC, even one armed with an Nvidia chip, will be an uphill climb.  Amid a memory shortage, it could hardly be a worse moment to be launching a new PC platform.

content.title: Even Nvidia Will Struggle to Save the PC
content.summary: Chip stocks, led by Intel and  Qualcomm  fell sharply, while PC stocks, led by  Dell Technologies  and  HP  jumped.  A new kind of PC, even one armed with an Nvidia chip, will be an uphill climb.  Amid a memory shortage, it could hardly be a worse moment to be launching a new PC platform.
